items.title: USDA posts 2026 timeline for SNAP food-restriction waivers in multiple states
items.summary: USDA's Food and Nutrition Service lists approved state waivers that restrict SNAP purchases of items like soda, energy drinks, and candy. The page shows target implementation dates including Texas (Apr. 1, 2026), Florida (Apr. 20, 2026), and Colorado (Apr. 30, 2026), with additional states scheduled later in 2026.

items.title: CMS issues Medicaid/CHIP managed care monitoring and oversight bulletin with new reporting timing
items.summary: CMS reminded states they must monitor managed care plans across at least 14 program areas under 42 CFR §438.66(b) and use monitoring data to improve plan performance. The bulletin highlights upcoming data/reporting timing, including reporting new T-MSIS state-directed payment (SDP) fields as soon as possible but no later than September 2026.

items.title: HUD rescinds and revises multiple COVID-era PIH notices affecting Housing Choice Voucher and public housing operations
items.summary: HUD Notice PIH 2026-06 (issued March 16, 2026) rescinds numerous COVID-era PIH notices and amends prior guidance, including changes related to remote hearings/briefings provisions. HUD notes that while certain COVID-era guidance is withdrawn, PHAs still must comply with federal civil rights and language access requirements.
items.source: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)

items.title: HHS/ACF opens comment period to extend LIHEAP Quarterly Reports information collection
items.summary: The Administration for Children and Families requested public comment on a 3-year extension of the LIHEAP Quarterly Reports (OMB No. 0970-0589). Comments are due April 3, 2026, and proposed changes would move carryover/reallotment data elements into the quarterly report to reduce redundant reporting.

items.title: USDA temporarily increases WIC maximum monthly milk allowance through Sept. 30, 2026
items.summary: USDA's WIC Policy Memorandum #2026-1 updates the maximum monthly allowance (MMA) of fluid milk for FY 2026, with amounts including 16 quarts for Food Packages IV A/B and VI, 22 quarts for Food Package V A/B, and 24 quarts for Food Package VII. USDA says the temporary amounts must be maintained through September 30, 2026.

items.title: IRS: most EITC and Additional Child Tax Credit refunds expected by March 2, 2026
items.summary: The IRS says most refunds for filers claiming the Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC) and the Additional Child Tax Credit (ACTC) should be available by March 2, 2026 for taxpayers using direct deposit and with no other issues. The agency also says its Where's My Refund tool will show projected deposit dates for most early EITC/ACTC filers by Feb. 21, 2026.

items.title: SSA posts key 2026 SSI/SSDI work and benefit thresholds (SGA, TWP, SSI federal benefit rate)
items.summary: SSA's 2026 Red Book update lists the SSI federal benefit rate as $994/month for an eligible individual and $1,491/month for an eligible couple. It also lists 2026 Substantial Gainful Activity (SGA) levels at $1,690/month (non-blind) and $2,830/month (blind), and the Trial Work Period amount at $1,210/month.

items.title: USDA publishes SNAP FY 2026 cost-of-living adjustments
items.summary: USDA's SNAP FY 2026 cost-of-living adjustments page posts updated benefit parameters for the fiscal year, including maximum allotments and deductions used to calculate SNAP benefits. These COLA updates typically take effect with the new fiscal year on Oct. 1, and states use them to update benefit calculations.

items.title: MACPAC releases March 2026 Report to Congress on Medicaid and CHIP
items.summary: MACPAC's March 2026 Report to Congress includes a recommendation to promote the home- and community-based services (HCBS) workforce by requiring states to report hourly wages paid to HCBS workers. The report also covers behavioral health in Medicaid/CHIP and Medicaid's role for justice-involved youth and children in foster care.

items.title: Reuters: U.S. Senate passes housing affordability bill; House vote next
items.summary: Reuters reports the Senate passed a housing affordability package 89-10 aimed at boosting affordable housing development and modernizing rural housing. The bill would also increase availability of federal block grants to states and raise loan limits for federally-backed multifamily mortgages; it next heads to the House.
